== Notes for [[Item:Q52560|Q52560]] ==

{geni:about_me} http://www.gloriousindia.com/history/dynasties/ikshvaku/index.html
<p>http://www.gloriousindia.com/history/dynasties/ikshvaku/purukutsa.html He married Nagkanya "Narmada". </p>
<p>--------------------</p>
<p>PURUKUTSA PERFORMED THE ASWAMEDHA YAJNA (HORSE SACRIFICE). HE MARRIED NAGAKANYA "NARMADA" . HE HELPED NAGAS IN THEIR WAR AGAINST THE GANDHARVAS.HARIDASA GOTRA LINAGE STARTS FROM HERE.bc</p>
<p>.........................................</p>
<p>5500BC</p>
<p>Källa: History of Ancient India: From 7300 BC to 4250 BC</p>
<p>http://www.sacred-texts.com/hin/vp/vp095.htm</p>
<p>>The son of Ambarísha, the son of Mándhátri, was Yuvanáśwa; his son was Harita, from whom the Angirasa Háritas were descended.</p>
<p>>Purukutsa had a son by Narmadá named Trasadasyu, whose son was Sambhúta 5, whose son was Anarańya, who was slain, by Rávańa in his triumphant progress through the nations. The son of Anarańya was Prishadaśwa; his son was Haryyaśwa; his son was Sumanas 6; his son was Tridhanwan; his son was Trayyáruńa; and his son was Satyavrata, who obtained the appellation of Triśanku, and was degraded to the condition of a Cháńd́ála, or outcast
<p>http://vaniquotes.org/wiki/Purukutsa</p>

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
